text: Biology
the study of life and living things
original_text: Biology is the study of life and living things.

text: explains how organisms grow, survive, reproduce, and interact
original_text: Biology explains how organisms grow, survive, reproduce, and interact.

text: all living things are made of one or more cells
original_text: All living things are made of one or more cells.

text: cells are the basic units of life
original_text: Cells are the basic units of life.

text: some organisms are made of only one cell
original_text: Some organisms are made of only one cell.

text: multicellular organisms are made of many specialized cells
original_text: Multicellular organisms are made of many specialized cells.

text: DNA carries genetic instructions
original_text: DNA carries genetic instructions.

text: genes are sections of DNA that influence traits
original_text: Genes are sections of DNA that influence traits.

text: proteins perform many important jobs inside cells
original_text: Proteins perform many important jobs inside cells.

text: enzymes help chemical reactions happen faster in living things
original_text: Enzymes help chemical reactions happen faster in living things.

text: organisms need energy to survive
original_text: Organisms need energy to survive.

text: plants capture energy from sunlight through photosynthesis
original_text: Plants capture energy from sunlight through photosynthesis.

text: animals get energy by consuming other organisms
original_text: Animals get energy by consuming other organisms.

text: cellular respiration releases usable energy from food molecules
original_text: Cellular respiration releases usable energy from food molecules.

text: metabolism includes all chemical processes inside an organism
original_text: Metabolism includes all chemical processes inside an organism.

text: homeostasis means maintaining stable internal conditions
original_text: Homeostasis means maintaining stable internal conditions.

text: body temperature regulation is an example of homeostasis
original_text: Body temperature regulation is an example of homeostasis.

text: organisms respond to changes in their environment
original_text: Organisms respond to changes in their environment.

text: reproduction allows life to continue across generations
original_text: Reproduction allows life to continue across generations.

text: asexual reproduction creates offspring from one parent
original_text: Asexual reproduction creates offspring from one parent.

text: sexual reproduction combines genetic information from two parents
original_text: Sexual reproduction combines genetic information from two parents.

text: variation helps populations adapt over time
original_text: Variation helps populations adapt over time.

text: natural selection favors traits that improve survival and reproduction
original_text: Natural selection favors traits that improve survival and reproduction.

text: evolution explains how populations change over generations
original_text: Evolution explains how populations change over generations.

text: adaptations are traits that help organisms survive in their environments
original_text: Adaptations are traits that help organisms survive in their environments.

text: ecosystems include living organisms and nonliving surroundings
original_text: Ecosystems include living organisms and nonliving surroundings.

text: food chains show how energy moves through organisms
original_text: Food chains show how energy moves through organisms.

text: food webs show many connected feeding relationships
original_text: Food webs show many connected feeding relationships.

text: producers make their own food
original_text: Producers make their own food.

text: consumers get energy by eating other organisms
original_text: Consumers get energy by eating other organisms.

text: decomposers recycle nutrients by breaking down dead material
original_text: Decomposers recycle nutrients by breaking down dead material.

text: bacteria can be helpful, harmful, or neutral
original_text: Bacteria can be helpful, harmful, or neutral.

text: fungi play important roles in decomposition
original_text: Fungi play important roles in decomposition.

text: viruses are not considered fully living by many biologists
original_text: Viruses are not considered fully living by many biologists.

text: viruses need host cells to reproduce
original_text: Viruses need host cells to reproduce.

text: the immune system protects the body from disease
original_text: The immune system protects the body from disease.

text: white blood cells help defend against infections
original_text: White blood cells help defend against infections.

text: vaccines train the immune system to recognize threats
original_text: Vaccines train the immune system to recognize threats.

text: antibiotics treat bacterial infections, not viral infections
original_text: Antibiotics treat bacterial infections, not viral infections.

text: disease can spread through air, water, contact, or living carriers
original_text: Disease can spread through air, water, contact, or living carriers.

text: the nervous system sends signals throughout the body
original_text: The nervous system sends signals throughout the body.

text: the brain coordinates many body functions
original_text: The brain coordinates many body functions.

text: neurons transmit information using electrical and chemical signals
original_text: Neurons transmit information using electrical and chemical signals.

text: the circulatory system transports oxygen, nutrients, and wastes
original_text: The circulatory system transports oxygen, nutrients, and wastes.

text: the heart pumps blood through the body
original_text: The heart pumps blood through the body.

text: the respiratory system exchanges oxygen and carbon dioxide
original_text: The respiratory system exchanges oxygen and carbon dioxide.

text: the digestive system breaks food into usable nutrients
original_text: The digestive system breaks food into usable nutrients.

text: the skeletal system supports and protects the body
original_text: The skeletal system supports and protects the body.

text: the muscular system allows movement
original_text: The muscular system allows movement.

text: the endocrine system uses hormones to send signals
original_text: The endocrine system uses hormones to send signals.

text: plants have roots, stems, and leaves with specialized functions
original_text: Plants have roots, stems, and leaves with specialized functions.

text: roots absorb water and minerals from soil
original_text: Roots absorb water and minerals from soil.

text: leaves capture sunlight for photosynthesis
original_text: Leaves capture sunlight for photosynthesis.

text: stems support plants and transport materials
original_text: Stems support plants and transport materials.

text: flowers are involved in reproduction for many plants
original_text: Flowers are involved in reproduction for many plants.

text: chlorophyll helps plants absorb light energy
original_text: Chlorophyll helps plants absorb light energy.

text: mitochondria release energy from food molecules
original_text: Mitochondria release energy from food molecules.

text: chloroplasts carry out photosynthesis in plant cells
original_text: Chloroplasts carry out photosynthesis in plant cells.

text: the cell membrane controls what enters and leaves a cell
original_text: The cell membrane controls what enters and leaves a cell.

text: the nucleus stores genetic information in many cells
original_text: The nucleus stores genetic information in many cells.

text: ribosomes help build proteins
original_text: Ribosomes help build proteins.

text: cytoplasm fills much of the inside of a cell
original_text: Cytoplasm fills much of the inside of a cell.

text: plant cells have cell walls for extra support
original_text: Plant cells have cell walls for extra support.

text: animal cells do not have cell walls
original_text: Animal cells do not have cell walls.

text: microscopes allow scientists to study cells
original_text: Microscopes allow scientists to study cells.

text: classification organizes living things into groups
original_text: Classification organizes living things into groups.

text: species are groups of organisms that can usually reproduce with each other
original_text: Species are groups of organisms that can usually reproduce with each other.

text: taxonomy helps scientists name and classify organisms
original_text: Taxonomy helps scientists name and classify organisms.

text: biodiversity describes the variety of life in an area
original_text: Biodiversity describes the variety of life in an area.

text: high biodiversity can make ecosystems more resilient
original_text: High biodiversity can make ecosystems more resilient.

text: mutations are changes in genetic material
original_text: Mutations are changes in genetic material.

text: some mutations are harmful, some are helpful, and many are neutral
original_text: Some mutations are harmful, some are helpful, and many are neutral.

text: inheritance passes traits from parents to offspring
original_text: Inheritance passes traits from parents to offspring.

text: dominant and recessive traits describe patterns of inheritance
original_text: Dominant and recessive traits describe patterns of inheritance.

text: punnett squares can predict possible genetic outcomes
original_text: Punnett squares can predict possible genetic outcomes.

text: ecology studies relationships between organisms and environments
original_text: Ecology studies relationships between organisms and environments.

text: habitats provide organisms with resources they need
original_text: Habitats provide organisms with resources they need.

text: A niche describes an organism's role in an ecosystem
original_text: A niche describes an organism's role in an ecosystem.

text: competition occurs when organisms need the same limited resources
original_text: Competition occurs when organisms need the same limited resources.

text: symbiosis describes close relationships between different species
original_text: Symbiosis describes close relationships between different species.

text: predation affects both predator and prey populations
original_text: Predation affects both predator and prey populations.

original_text: Carrying capacity is the maximum population an environment can support.
